tokens: Okay, and Geno, we'd like to vary the parking a little bit because of this.
We'd like to try and get those batteries cooled down.
We'd like to have you park about 60 feet north of the Central Station.
And then we'll open the battery covers.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, I can't read you, but "facing east" and "down-Sun" are not the same.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're on the Rover, aren't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got it, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got your block coming, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, it doesn't take much to get those battery covers dirty.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Gene, did you copy me that we meant facing up-Sun?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) (To himself as he fastens his seat belt) What did I do, get fatter?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the shorting plug meter is 90 percent scale to the right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, give me that parking heading again, would you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we'd like you to park facing the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: About 60 feet north of the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Sixty feet north of Central Station.
I can't park a little northeast?
Now, okay, and you want the battery covers open?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And that means you will have to dust them before you open them.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Man, am I glad I didn't land up here, Jack!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, ALSEP is connected; RTG is connected.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is that where you're going to have the Central Station, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, Geno, that's the best I can do without spending a lot more time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And let me talk to you about it.
I asked them about this depression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Your (heat flow) probes are all right out in here.

And if you get in the bottom of it for the...either this one, or go out there - essentially in the straight line between you and me now - (there's) another depression (that) would be good for the neutron flux.
(Pause) You need to be over that way...you're just a...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, they want me to park about here where...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You need to be over here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Where's Central Station, right there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I've got to park in the Sun for the batteries.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How does it look behind me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, 17, for your planning, we're now about 20 minutes behind the timeline.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Talking to Gene, who is maneuvering the Rover) That's good, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Roger, we're about twenty minutes behind the timeline, two-zero minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I guess it could be worse, considering a couple of things.
Okay, about time I got those batteries.
Okay, Jack, let me give you this first, so I can get to work.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm sorry, I forgot you had it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're aligned on the high gain.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me get these (battery) covers dusted.
It's not as hard to get at the covers as one might think.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; I'm working on the LEAM connection now, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, it's hard to hit that garbage pile.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Understand you also have the heat flow connector connected by now?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm a little late, Jack, 'til I get these battery covers opened.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got the heat flow for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, the old LEAM connector doesn't connect - just like usual - or lock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm not going to touch the batteries.
The covers are clean, and the batteries themselves are clean.
The LCRU has been dusted and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The battery covers are in the shade.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to go to Max (cooling) for a minute here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It seems hot in the valley of Taurus...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'm going back to Intermediate.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm ready to go to work.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to push the gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's blinking, and remember what I said, it's a piece of cake to take on and off (the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is north over here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You (that is, Jack) got the connector connected.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, man, all I could do is go downhill over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm just trying to level (the Central Station)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Are you going to move that very much?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I'm just...I'll be working with it to try to level it.
That's going to be a major task.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, there's 30 feet, I'll get this thing squared away when I...Okay, Boyd-bolt time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, these things are just like they are at the Cape.
You can feel every one of them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, has Ron been able to see the LM?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll straighten it up in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I hope this does the things that we want it to for (us).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I'm not doing too badly on keeping things clean.
The base of the Central Station (has) got some stuff (dust) on it, but, otherwise, it's pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We appreciate your efforts, and we understand you got the LEAM connected eventually.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yes, and it locked; (it) just took some diddling.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, I never drilled a hole where there's not a can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, did you give me an answer?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Having missed Jack's most recent question) Roger.
Ron thinks he has seen it (the LM).
We haven't had a confirmation on the last orbit when you were talking to him, but he thinks he saw it the previous orbit.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Can the base of the LSG be touching the soil?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, it's very soft and it's going to be very hard to level...(Stops to listen to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The base can be touching the ground.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's leveled, aligned, the sunshield is shaded inside.
The level bubble is just touching the outer circle, or the "one" circle.
It's perfectly centered now and I'm going to uncage.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, it's uncaged; the gimbal is swinging.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Jack, you're still in Max...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The bubble is back just touching...(Listens) Yeah, I know...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I went to "pseudo intermediate," between Minimum and Intermediate.
The (level) bubble (on the LSG) is just touching the circle, and the Sun alignment is good.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You've got some good slack here (in the cables), if you can leave it that way.
You shouldn't have the cable draped across anything; that's good.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to try and get this thing in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a little dust in this mirror, though.
I want to make sure I...Bob, I got a little dust on the white surface - not on the mirror - of the heat flow (electronics package).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Stand by on that, Jack - (correcting himself) - Gene.
Is that on the heat reflector?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, it's on the north side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As long as it's not on the mirror, it's okay, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, let me take another look.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Give me some more slack up here, you're draped.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This way just a little, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, we sure didn't need blocks or anything out here.
There's enough soil here to level almost anything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But it's so soft, though, it's hard to get a best level.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I did something wrong.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Pulled the pin at the wrong time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The heat flow (electronics package) is level; the gnomon is good.
And, Bob, I verified that that dirt is not on the mirror.
It's on the white stuff that, you know, is horizontal to the (top) surface of the box.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The camera's been running all this time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Your temperatures are down to 100 and maybe a skosh under 120, so maybe those batteries are cooling off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't want to walk on that third EVA.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm getting to like driving this machine.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Pin 2 always comes after...(fixing his joke) comes before pin 1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think that's in the NASA documents now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I think I overdid that one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, it went clean out of sight.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Laughing) Oh, you think you're so clever.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Believe it or not, Bob, I'm anchoring the geophone module.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Jack, it looks to us on the TV as though you're anchoring the geophone module with a flag.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pretending neither of them is hearing the other) I'm anchoring the geophone module with a flag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; one leg, two legs, three legs.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, remind me to police the garbage pile.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The garbage pile is turning out to be just like every other ALSEP deployment.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I understand all your legs came out okay, Geno, or didn't come out.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Answering Bob) Yes, sir; they all came out okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got my tools of the trade right here.
I'm ready to go to work.
Now, I put a mark in the deck...(Looking for a spot he scratched on the ground to show where he wanted to drill the first hole) There it is; right there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, what have I forgotten?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Try rotating the UHT (Universal Handling Tool).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, don't worry about my garbage pile.
It turns out it looks very much like the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I made a mark over here that says that should be about cable length...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What am I doing over here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I'm going right in here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, this will be all right.
I just want to keep away from you there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We shall soon see how tuned (sic) we are.
I'm anxious to see what's under this mantle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, Bob, I hope I can drill you a couple of good holes...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, the arrow (on the LMS) is east/west, pointing west.
The bubble is in the center.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I'm lucky, it'll stay there.
I'm trying to keep a little further away from Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, that was my mark (on the ground).
I'd sure hate to drill a hole that was outside of the length of the cable.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, the LMS is deployed.
The screen is over the port.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to move one big rock.
(Pause) What am I doing down in here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What were you doing down in there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And Bob, there's a little bit of dust adhering to the sides of the LMS.
And a few particles (covering), oh, less than half a percent of the surface on the top.
But, of course, you're going to clean that one off (by removing the screen); so that's all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The north side has about a 10 or 15 percent dust cover(ing).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Geno, you're leaning pretty heavy forward on that drill.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: She's going in like she's in some pretty dense stuff, and then I hit some rock here.
I'll watch it; I won't lean forward.
I'm not putting too much pressure on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It sounds to me like she's chipping away through rock.
(Pause) May be just a little-longer-drilling hole than it was at the Cape.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, she's going in; but not without a little bit of resistance.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Every once in a while she breaks through a soft spot.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I'll tell you, this Central Station's a bear - a bear - to get level.
(Pause) Well, I just got dust on it now.
(Pause) It (meaning the ground)'s just too soft.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, that sure was drilling in hard stuff because it took a lot to get it (the drill) off (the stem).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we could certainly stand (tolerate) a little bit of dust, at least, on top of that Central Station sunshield.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I guess the level's important.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can just see what John (Young)'s thinking right now.
That's where you expend your energy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I don't know that I'm going to be able to do that without a lot of time.
It (meaning the level bubble)'s hanging against the south edge.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know whether I'm going to be able to level the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Without looking) Anything I can do, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got it off the edge.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; maybe we better just leave it there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'm making it worse by getting dust on the top.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, the dust on the top is not as important as getting it leveled, Jack.
But if you get it (the bubble) broken off the edge, that ought to be good enough.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I'm riding at about 3.82 (psi suit pressure).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got, oh, I guess about 80...Well, no; (counting gauge marks) there's half.
I guess, 60 percent (oxygen); no flags and no tones.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's touching the second ring; the gnomon is aligned, and I'm going to leave it alone.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I think I lost all the time I might have made up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, it's obvious that I'm going through some pretty tough stuff - (through) consolidated material, like rock fragments - and then it breaks through; and then it jumps for about 3 or 4 inches and then I hit some more fragments.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got too low on that one.
I thought I had that gauged.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 4) geophone, you'll go out of sight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, there would be absolutely no way of breaking this drill from those bores without that tool, I guarantee you that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I found a way to get this off, though, with a little help.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The third and last one on this hole.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: My fender's still on; which makes me happy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll tell you, if you could come and sprinkle the whole area with water and get rid of some of this dust...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And can you remember if those heat flow cables are not crossed as they come out of that box?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I very definitely made a point of not crossing them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughs; then assumes an oratorical tone) Oh, ho, ho, ho.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This leveling is really throwing me behind the power curve; but I know they're serious about it, so, if I can keep it where it is now, I'm "in like Flynn".
Even the gnomon is aligned within a shadow width.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Really working down there, are you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm in something tough down there now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, what bore are you in?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I let go of that drill, and it kept running...If that drill kept running and I wasn't anchored to the ground, (he chuckles) it would throw me over the Massif.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I'm in the motherlode down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, if it's getting really tough and you're not making much progress, we'll be happy with it where it is.
(Pause) Well, it looks like you really...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, sir, you're going to be happy with it where it's supposed to be; and that's where it's...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You were hiding it from us...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We couldn't tell how deep in you were.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: They're going in all the way, and they're both going to work.
(Pause) Gets a little tough looking into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Putting the drill on the ground) I don't know where I picked that word up (Jack chuckles), but it's better than some (pause), I guess.
Now if I can use my little lean-tool here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Put this out of the way.
(Pause; putting the drill about ten feet north) Bob, I'm into the white marks; it depends on what you want to call the surface.
You know, give or take 6 or 8 inches.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, something like that will do, Gene, I guess.
We can measure it (garbled, but means "from the photos").
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, is the stuff (the drill cuttings) coming up changing color on you at all?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't even tell where it's coming up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing as the top of the Central Station springs up; Gene turns to look) Ho ho.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Looking back at his work) I don't think it (the cuttings) is coming up.
I think I'm just pushing it aside.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I saw something come up then just then, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, it deploys itself; it turns out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I finally leveled it in the bull's eye.
I don't know whether you heard me or not, but it was perfect.
It's got about 20 percent dust cover on the top.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Sure glad you've got that probe covered.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, ALSEP says that that's okay; that twenty percent's no problem.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) I put a rock under the northwest corner.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The old geophone cable caught on the corner.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To himself) That says F4B on that; that's an airplane!
(Long Pause; he discards something) Houston, there's no dust on the (heat flow) probe except that which was on my hands.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sorry not to be more talkative, but this is taking all the concentration I got.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to have to push this down, I can see that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, this Velcro doesn't hold any better here than it did in training.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Talking to himself as he reaches for something on the rack) Oh, don't lose that, Geno.
Notice how you talk to yourself out here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "You is getting farther and farther behind."
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I've had my "whoops" for today.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's funny how for every action there's an equal and opposite reaction, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How far behind am I, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're showing Gene just about 20 minutes; and Jack just about 25.
Between 20 and 25 (minutes) for both of you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, if this thing isn't going to work better than that!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, just like the book says, it's down to Papa 1; and it hooked.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The old fingers really suffer on these.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now this one down to F1.
Would you believe (it's down to) F1?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, in this soil, best number I can give you is about an inch below the white spots, or Bravo 1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a better way of putting that last thermal shield on, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, you're looking at it coming out to the south, but I don't expect it'll stay that way unless I put some dirt over the cable.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You like that thermal shield the way it is?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Dirt's okay, if you want to put it on there, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll tell you, I'm happy with it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, the thermal shield is on there, Bob.
I got them all on there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If you want to put some dirt on there to hold it down, that's okay.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I got it down without the dirt.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm just finding all sorts of good ways to make life easier out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) Stay away from the cables.
(To Bob) Bob, and I didn't forget the last measurement either.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, can you see this big mound that's just...(correcting himself) not a mound but the depression that's just to the north of me?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, how's that look for the core?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) Geno, can you give us a distance estimate to that?
Does it look like it's 80 feet or so (from the RTG)?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, is there any way a level bubble can fail?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Jack, remember that's on top of those wobbly springs there.
And with the thing not being straight, you shouldn't really expect the level bubble to be level after the thing's been deployed.
That happened at the Cape a couple of times, remember?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah; but Bob, I've moved this practically all the way down the full throw and that bubble won't move, and I can't get it to move by tilting it; and I was leveled.
And the bubble on the top of the Central Station is still level.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're talking about the level on your other one, huh?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) I can't get it to move to the other side of the fluid.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That bubble's stuck in there, somehow.
That's not even pointing close to the Earth.
Okay, I'm going to have to tweak it up...Let them see the signal strength and tweak it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you try and manually point it; try and level it and see what you can do toward getting it (pointed) towards the Earth.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I can't figure that one out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just go ahead and use the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Maybe I jarred it loose here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I jarred it loose.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I got the other one loose.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...If you're looking at me, what I'm talking about is this depression in here for the core.
Oh, maybe 15, 20 meters out in here.
Jack, what did you have in mind for the neutron flux?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Either the one you're down in, there; or next one over behind that rock in front of you over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh yeah, I can go way over there.
(Grasping the drill) That's not too far probably for...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, either way I think is fine, Gene.
But I would suggest behind the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I thought they wanted a core in that depression.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there's also one over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can give them a choice.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll go behind that rock; that looks good from here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, 17, we think you guys are in by far the best position to judge that; far better than we are.
You know what the requirements are on shielding and...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...(you know that the distance from the RTG needs to be) greater than 50 meters...(Correcting himself) 25 meters.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Looked like that one went in fairly well.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, probably about like the other one did.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, I must be getting old.
I expect the next two are going to be a little harder.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I'm not very happy with this (antenna) level.
Have me come back a little bit later, when they've warmed up some more, and let's see what it looks like.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give me a mark when you turn it on, and we'll see what kind of signals we get.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The gnomon (shadow) is aligned; I'm going to turn the shorting plug on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the needle is full scale left.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, that whole bore turns in the ground, it's so loose.
You know how those threads sometimes stick on you a little bit.
(Getting the wrench again) I got one stuck halfway down and the whole bore is turning, so now I've got to use a wrench on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I've got a rock about 10 feet southeast of my LEAM location.
I can move a little more north and get, oh, 15 feet from that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How big is the rock there, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, it stands...It's a meter wide and stands about a third of a meter high.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: About a third...about a foot high?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Lecturing tone) A third of a meter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, how's that for soil mechanics?
I pulled the first bore right on out trying to get this thing on right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, put it in before your hole fills up there, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Right now I'm interested in getting this second bore on.
(Pause) Now, let's see if I can get it back in!
(Pause) Well, not quite as far, but high enough for me to reach the (drill handle).
It still feels, Bob, like there's a lot of fragmental material down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That was an interesting little exercise.
Well, I got the bore on right, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As long as it's only 1 foot high and 10 feet away, that's satisfactory.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, the LEAM's deployed, aligned, and the level bubble is just touching the inner ring.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Wanting to know if Houston is getting data from the Central Station) Hey, Bob.
Did you get anything from the ALSEP yet?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We started to tell you that when you had the question there, and we're getting a good lockup on the data.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, keep an eye on it (that is, on the signal from the Central Station), because I'm not happy with the level.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll get back with you on that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) I found a way to get over cables.
(He makes a little jumping noise as he kangaroo-hops over the cable) Bee-doop!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get this one off and take a bite of candy here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm kind of having trouble with UHTs today.
They just don't want to lock in when you get dust in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Be careful with that UHT on the heat flow because it was aligned, real good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The heat flow electronics, when you go over there for that UHT, was aligned.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I'm going to take a zap of cold (cooling) water.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Almost looks like it's getting dark out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing and doing an Amos and Andy impression) Hope not, or "we is in trouble".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I may have gone the wrong way.
I went to Min instead of Max.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's coming out a little hard.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: By any chance (do you) have any heat flow data yet?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We don't have the heat flow turned on yet.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm about to give you your number...Oh, God darn it!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself as he runs the drill for a second) Crank it a couple of times.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, just like I thought; the antenna doesn't want to go in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is that the number 3 section there, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, it's the last one I got.
(Chuckles) I guess we'll find out when I put the probe in.
I think they're all in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm about ready to deploy some geophones.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you get the antenna into that subpallet okay, eventually?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I occasionally hit stuff and it spits this whole drill back at me.
Knocks it back about a half an inch or so, and then it will bite through it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My general impression is that there is an awful lot of fragments I'm busting up down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll tell you, Bob, (in) that last 6 inches I really came into something hard; but it's down all the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
